title: Ascend NPU SchedulerOutput Missing grammar_bitmask Attribute Guard Pattern
symptom: AttributeError: 'SchedulerOutput' object has no attribute 'grammar_bitmask' during vLLM serving on Ascend NPU. The serving engine crashes at model_runner_v1.py lines 1709 and 2018 when accessing scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask directly on Ascend builds where the SchedulerOutput implementation differs from CUDA.
root_cause: On Ascend NPU builds of vLLM, the SchedulerOutput object may not expose the grammar_bitmask attribute that exists on CUDA builds. The Ascend scheduler implementation may omit grammar-constrained decoding data or use a different internal structure. Direct attribute access without defensive guards raises AttributeError and crashes the serving engine. The upstream gpu_model_runner.py avoids this by using a utility function (apply_grammar_bitmask from vllm.v1.structured_output.utils) with a separate grammar_output tracking variable, rather than accessing scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask directly.
fix_steps: 1. Locate all direct attribute accesses to scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask in vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py via grep: `scheduler_output\.grammar_bitmask`.

2. At the attribute retrieval site (inside apply_grammar_bitmask method), replace direct access `scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask` with defensive retrieval: `getattr(scheduler_output, 'grammar_bitmask', None)`.
3. When the result is None, the method should handle gracefully — the downstream XGrammar apply_token_bitmask_inplace call must be skipped.

4. At the call site (the conditional check before apply_grammar_bitmask), replace direct conditional `scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask is not None` with double guard: `hasattr(scheduler_output, 'grammar_bitmask') and scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask is not None`.
5. When the attribute is missing or None, skip grammar-constrained decoding entirely — logits pass through unchanged.

6. Apply the same two-layer defensive pattern to any other SchedulerOutput attribute that may differ between CUDA and Ascend builds (e.g., scheduled_spec_decode_tokens, structured_output_request_ids).
7. Run lsp_diagnostics on the modified file and verify no new errors introduced.
8. Test with a serving launch that does NOT use grammar-constrained decoding to confirm the guard prevents crash when attribute is absent.
affected_patterns: scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask (direct attribute access — crashes on Ascend)
if scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask is not None: (direct conditional check — crashes on Ascend)

code_changes.before:
if scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask is not None:
    logits = self.apply_grammar_bitmask(scheduler_output, logits)
code_changes.after:
if hasattr(scheduler_output, 'grammar_bitmask') and scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask is not None:
    logits = self.apply_grammar_bitmask(scheduler_output, logits)

references: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py#L2914-L2917 — upstream pattern: if grammar_output is not None rather than scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask
antipatterns: Do NOT assume SchedulerOutput exposes the same attributes as CUDA builds — always use defensive accessors on Ascend.
Do NOT access scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask without hasattr/getattr guard — this crashes the serving engine.
Do NOT crash or raise when grammar_bitmask is unavailable — skip grammar-constrained decoding gracefully and allow normal token sampling to proceed.

Do NOT only add the getattr guard without also adding the hasattr guard at the call site — the method body downstream code (np.zeros_like, enumerate over structured_output_request_ids) will also crash.

meta.refiner_reasoning: Candidate classified this as 'prompt' type, but the fix is a concrete, verifiable code change (defensive getattr/hasattr guards at two call sites in model_runner_v1.py) that is already applied and can be followed blindly by another code_adapter agent. This qualifies as a skill — it prescribes a repeatable two-layer defensive coding pattern for any SchedulerOutput attribute that may differ between CUDA and Ascend NPU builds. The upstream gpu_model_runner.py avoids this issue entirely by using a separate grammar_output variable rather than accessing scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask, confirming that the attribute is indeed CUDA-specific. Confidence raised from 0.75 to 0.85 because the fix is verified in place with zero residual direct accesses and both guard layers are present.
